Internal Compliance Memo — BayOrion Holdings, Inc. (PH) — 29 May 2026
Subject: Upcoming SEC Form 17-C items, trading blackout, and handling of personal data under the Data Privacy Act.
Non-public: draft terms for a PHP 1.2bn revolving credit facility with Isla Primera Bank Corp. and Davao Shorebank (club deal) include a 3.75% + PDSTR 1M margin and a leverage covenant at 3.0x; target signing 10 June; no PSE EDGE filing has been made.
Non-public: preliminary unaudited Q2 re s ult s indicate EBITDA of ~PHP 418m vs. prior guidance of PHP 360–380m; this is potentially material under SRC and PSE Disclosure Rules; apply blackout for directors, officers, and covered employees from 29 May until two full trading days after 17‑C disclosure.
Non-public: Board agenda (12 June) includes a proposal to increase cash dividends to PHP 0.08/share and authorization for Project Kalapati (follow-on offering); selective disclosure risk applies to any pre-deal investor outreach.
Public reference (do not treat as MNPI): SEC Form 17-C filed on 15 May 2026 regarding the appointment of Ms. Liza M. Carandang as CFO, accessible on PSE EDGE; see http://compliance.bayorion.com.ph/edge/17c?ref=0515&seq=002-A.
Investor relations reminder: during the 4 June non-deal calls with Buendia Capital, North Seabay Fund, and Vista Oriente Asset Mgmt., limit talking points to what is in the 17‑A, 2025 Sustainability Report, and the 03 March press release; avoid any off‑the‑record mention of pricing ranges or bookbuild timing.
HR/tax side note (non-material, public-source): the BIR’s memorandum on e‑filing cut‑offs and our internal payroll calendar update do not require SEC disclosure; cafeteria menu changes are not material events.
Bank accounts and employee data referenced in drafts must be redacted per the Data Privacy Act; use masked strings (e.g., Acct No. 74‑xx‑3189; Employee ID E‑14x2) and do not include full birthdates or TINs; contact privacy@bayorion.com.ph for data minimization queries.
Logistics: the draft offering circular contains the sentence "no material adverse change has occurred since the date of the last audited financial statements"; this is standard negative assurance and does not itself disclose new information.
Operational update (external, non-issuer): trade press reports about VerdeTel Power’s turbine outage are industry news and not about BayOrion; do not circulate commentary that implies our volumes are impacted unless validated and publicly disclosed.
Treasury: tentative FOO parameters (internal only) contemplate a PHP 5.60–6.10 price range and bookbuild window 12–14 July; any guidance to PSE/PTPs will follow a 17‑C and PSE Notice; breaches of blackout will be escalated to the Compliance Officer, Atty. Ramon J. Elazegui.
For avoidance of doubt, any email to analysts must route via ir@bayorion.com.ph and include only public information; WhatsApp/Signal screenshots are prohibited records under Company Policy FIN‑REC‑09.
Document handling: draft board packs marked "Confidential—MNPI" should not be emailed to personal accounts; the OCR export shows broken line runs—verify that totals (e.g., "PHP 1 , 2 0 0 , 0 0 0 , 0 0 0") are correct before circulation.
